free_text: Experimental yields for W-nat irradiated with 2.6-GeV
- Fe targets. Entry superseded, see O1727
LAHET, CEM95, CEM2K, Cascade, INUCL and Yieldx
The experimental errors are within approx.6-35%
The errors in the independent and cumulative yields of
the reaction products for separate gamma-lines, were
determined using the error transfer formulas.
The uncertainties in the nuclear data-absolute values
of gamma-abundances and monitor reaction cross section
were found in the relevant analysis to make the major
version of the ISTC report, which is published as INDC
-By compiler. If a nucleus has isomer state, but data
heading  "isomer" contains space only, production cross
section includes isomer state production too.
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
- By author. Cumulative cross section is usual sum of
a nuclide independent cross section and cumulative
Production cross sections for Y-87, Sb-115, Er-159,
determination of the cross section see in ISTC-839B-99.
